description: Bulk knowledge ingestion pipeline. Create a Python script that generates .mk knowledge files from structured data. Add 5,000-10,000 facts across categories: science, math, geography, history, literature, music, sports, medicine, law, economics, philosophy, art, food, languages.
steps: Create MK_OS/tools/generate_knowledge.py - a Python script that programmatically generates .mk format facts across many categories. Use hardcoded structured data (dictionaries, lists) rather than downloading from the internet. Categories: elements (118), countries (195+capitals+continents), planets, human body, math constants, programming languages, famous scientists, historical events, animals (expanded), foods, sports, musical instruments, world religions, literary works, etc.

Run the Python script to generate multiple .mk files in ai_core/hre/knowledge_files/: generated_science.mk, generated_geography.mk, generated_history.mk, generated_culture.mk, generated_math.mk, generated_nature.mk, generated_technology.mk, generated_language.mk
Ensure each file follows the format: source|relation|target|weight (one fact per line, with # comments for section headers)
Target: at least 7,000 new facts total across all generated files
Verify pattern_graph.cpp can load the new files by confirming its loadAllKnowledge() scans the knowledge_files/ directory
Run make && make test to confirm the system compiles and loads expanded knowledge without issues
acceptance_criteria: At least 7,000 new facts generated across 8+ .mk files
make compiles successfully with expanded knowledge
make test passes all existing tests
